mnemonics.title: Kappa vs Lambda
mnemonics.content: KAPPA: Keep All Processes Parallel Always (stream-only); LAMBDA: Layered Architecture with Multiple Batch/stream Data Approaches

acronyms.usage: Used in fraud detection to identify multi-stream patterns

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a floating marker in a river indicating 'no data older than this point will arrive'

analogies.analogy: Like a highway toll system processing each vehicle immediately as it passes, rather than counting all cars at day's end
